misc.title: I finally did it. After years of hunting, rebuilding, replacing missing pieces, selling extras, trading, and slowly piecing everything back together, I completed my 100% CIB Nintendo produced Zelda co
misc.text: I finally did it. After years of hunting, rebuilding, replacing missing pieces, selling extras, trading, and slowly piecing everything back together, I completed my 100% CIB Nintendo produced Zelda collection. Zelda has always been my favorite Nintendo franchise, and it’s right there with Final Fantasy as one of my favorite series of all time. My love for Zelda goes way back to watching my mom play the original NES game when I was little. I was too young to fully understand what was happening, but the sound effects and especially the music all stuck with me. Then my cousin got an SNES with A Link to the Past and I spent so many weekends at his house completely blown away by that game. That was probably the moment I went from liking video games to being a video game fan for life. That’s why physical media still matters so much to me. Digital can’t really replace the feeling of holding something that carries that many memories. This collection is more than just games to me. It’s a tribute to my childhood, my family, my friendships, and all the memories that made me fall in love with gaming in the first place. My goal was to finish it in time for Zelda’s 40th anniversary year, and I pulled it off.

misc.text: Day 340: #9, 2004. “Eyeshield 21” Cover & Lead Color Pages (don’t know celebration) LCP also has a “where’s Waldo” mini game where you have to find the Devil Bat. Other Color Pages go to “Buso Renkin” issue also contains chapter 2 of “Steel Ball Run” TheMangaAddict ²³ CR: Golden Kamuy (@Th3MangaAddict) Day 439: #12, 2021. “Undead Unluck” Cover & Lead Color Pages to Celebrate the 1st Year Anniversary. Other Color Pages go to “Dr. Stone”, “Nine Dragons' Ball Parade” & a one shot called “Chou Nouryoku Inou-san” — https://nitter.net/Th3MangaAddict/status/2071263411523232048#m

items.title: I finally did it. After years of hunting, rebuilding, replacing missing pieces, selling extras, trading, and slowly piecing everything back together, I completed my 100% CIB Nintendo produced Zelda co
items.text: I finally did it. After years of hunting, rebuilding, replacing missing pieces, selling extras, trading, and slowly piecing everything back together, I completed my 100% CIB Nintendo produced Zelda collection. Zelda has always been my favorite Nintendo franchise, and it’s right there with Final Fantasy as one of my favorite series of all time. My love for Zelda goes way back to watching my mom play the original NES game when I was little. I was too young to fully understand what was happening, but the sound effects and especially the music all stuck with me. Then my cousin got an SNES with A Link to the Past and I spent so many weekends at his house completely blown away by that game. That was probably the moment I went from liking video games to being a video game fan for life. That’s why physical media still matters so much to me. Digital can’t really replace the feeling of holding something that carries that many memories. This collection is more than just games to me. It’s a tribute to my childhood, my family, my friendships, and all the memories that made me fall in love with gaming in the first place. My goal was to finish it in time for Zelda’s 40th anniversary year, and I pulled it off.
